title: The Commodities Jigsaw Puzzle: Recap & Outlook
section_path: The Commodities Jigsaw Puzzle: Recap & Outlook
section_path_text: The Commodities Jigsaw Puzzle: Recap & Outlook
summary:
Main
Image reference: RGB_COLOR_BRS_SHIPBROKERS_MONOGRAM_LOGO.png | assets/2025-03-04_the-commodities-jigsaw-puzzle-recap-amp-outlook_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_ee79606c8e74.png
Seaborne Iron Ore Changes Breakdown
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-03-04 121525.png | assets/2025-03-04_the-commodities-jigsaw-puzzle-recap-amp-outlook_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_5e09da83ab50.png
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-03-04 121539.png | assets/2025-03-04_the-commodities-jigsaw-puzzle-recap-amp-outlook_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_2f8b8fc94d44.png
In 2024, seaborne iron ore volumes experienced a year-on-year (y-o-y) increase of 2.4%, while ton-days expanded at a higher rate of 3.4%. This disproportionate growth in ton-days was primarily driven by an increase in longer-haul shipments from Brazil (+18.3 mln mt) and non-Pacific countries such as Peru (+13.3%), Ukraine (+330%), Mozambique (+17.4%) which saw non-pacific flows rise by 15.3 mln mt. Meanwhile, shorter-haul shipments from Australia to the Far East rose by 9.7 mln mt.
